mnemonics: Regression: Remember, Every Graph Really Illustrates Slope, Trends, Outliers, and Noise.
keyPoints: Regression analysis predicts a continuous outcome variable based on one or more predictor variables.
The goal is to find the line (or hyperplane) of best fit that minimizes the error between predicted and actual values.
It is used for forecasting and modeling the relationship between a dependent variable and one (simple) or more (multiple) independent variables.
commonMistakes: Confusing regression (predicting a continuous value) with classification (predicting a discrete category).
Failing to check for and address multicollinearity in multiple regression, which can make coefficient estimates unstable and difficult to interpret.
Assuming correlation implies causation; a regression model can show a relationship without proving one variable causes the other to change.
